dialogue.text: There is no doubt whatever about that.

dialogue.ja: There is no doubt whatever about that.

dialogue.text: Marley was the sole partner and co-propriitor of the counting house of Scrooge and Marley.

dialogue.ja: Marley was the sole partner and co-propriitor of the counting house of Scrooge and Marley.

dialogue.text: The sign still hung there and the firm was still known as Scrooge and Marley.
dialogue.zh: The sign still hung there and the firm was still known as Scrooge and Marley.
dialogue.ja: The sign still hung there and the firm was still known as Scrooge and Marley.

dialogue.text: But Marley was as dead as a doorknail.
dialogue.zh: But Marley was as dead as a doorknail.
dialogue.ja: But Marley was as dead as a doorknail.

dialogue.text: Ebenezer Scrooge, the surviving partner, was a man as hard and sharp as flipped and as solitary

dialogue.text: as an oyster.

dialogue.text: The cold within him froze his old features, nipped his nose, shriveled his cheek, stiffened

dialogue.ja: The cold within him froze his old features, nipped his nose, shriveled his cheek, stiffened

dialogue.text: his gait and spoke out shrewdly in his grating voice.
dialogue.zh: his gait and spoke out shrewdly in his grating voice.
dialogue.ja: his gait and spoke out shrewdly in his grating voice.

dialogue.text: Nobody ever stopped him in the street to say, my dear Scrooge, how are you?
dialogue.zh: Nobody ever stopped him in the street to say, my dear Scrooge, how are you?
dialogue.ja: Nobody ever stopped him in the street to say, my dear Scrooge, how are you?

dialogue.text: No beggars implored him to bestow a trifle.
dialogue.zh: No beggars implored him to bestow a trifle.
dialogue.ja: No beggars implored him to bestow a trifle.

dialogue.text: No children asked him the time and no man or woman ever asked him the way.
dialogue.zh: No children asked him the time and no man or woman ever asked him the way.
dialogue.ja: No children asked him the time and no man or woman ever asked him the way.

dialogue.text: He liked it thus.
dialogue.zh: He liked it thus.
dialogue.ja: He liked it thus.

dialogue.text: On Christmas Eve, a biting foggy day, Scrooge sat at his desk in the counting house.
dialogue.zh: On Christmas Eve, a biting foggy day, Scrooge sat at his desk in the counting house.
dialogue.ja: On Christmas Eve, a biting foggy day, Scrooge sat at his desk in the counting house.

dialogue.text: His clerk, Bob Cratchit, was in a dismal little cell, trying to warm himself in the small
dialogue.zh: His clerk, Bob Cratchit, was in a dismal little cell, trying to warm himself in the small
dialogue.ja: His clerk, Bob Cratchit, was in a dismal little cell, trying to warm himself in the small

dialogue.text: fire.
dialogue.zh: fire.
dialogue.ja: fire.

dialogue.text: Suddenly Scrooge's nephew Fred burst in, a merry Christmas uncle, God save you.
dialogue.zh: Suddenly Scrooge's nephew Fred burst in, a merry Christmas uncle, God save you.
dialogue.ja: Suddenly Scrooge's nephew Fred burst in, a merry Christmas uncle, God save you.

dialogue.text: Uncle, will you be joining us for dinner tonight?
dialogue.zh: Uncle, will you be joining us for dinner tonight?
dialogue.ja: Uncle, will you be joining us for dinner tonight?

dialogue.text: At least do it for the goodness and cheer of Christmas.
dialogue.zh: At least do it for the goodness and cheer of Christmas.
dialogue.ja: At least do it for the goodness and cheer of Christmas.

dialogue.text: Christmas is merely a time for paying bills without money and finding yourself a year
dialogue.zh: Christmas is merely a time for paying bills without money and finding yourself a year
dialogue.ja: Christmas is merely a time for paying bills without money and finding yourself a year

dialogue.text: old, but not an hour richer.
dialogue.zh: old, but not an hour richer.
dialogue.ja: old, but not an hour richer.

dialogue.text: If I had my will, every idiot who goes about with merry Christmas on his lips should be
dialogue.zh: If I had my will, every idiot who goes about with merry Christmas on his lips should be
dialogue.ja: If I had my will, every idiot who goes about with merry Christmas on his lips should be

dialogue.text: boiled with his own pudding and arrayed with a stake up holiday through his art.
dialogue.zh: boiled with his own pudding and arrayed with a stake up holiday through his art.
dialogue.ja: boiled with his own pudding and arrayed with a stake up holiday through his art.

dialogue.text: Don't waste any more of my time with these Christmas nonsense.
dialogue.zh: Don't waste any more of my time with these Christmas nonsense.
dialogue.ja: Don't waste any more of my time with these Christmas nonsense.

dialogue.text: Fred, seeing he was defeated, wished Bob Cratchit a merry Christmas and departed.
dialogue.zh: Fred, seeing he was defeated, wished Bob Cratchit a merry Christmas and departed.
dialogue.ja: Fred, seeing he was defeated, wished Bob Cratchit a merry Christmas and departed.

dialogue.text: Next, two portley gentlemen entered, soliciting donations for the poor and destitute.
dialogue.zh: Next, two portley gentlemen entered, soliciting donations for the poor and destitute.
dialogue.ja: Next, two portley gentlemen entered, soliciting donations for the poor and destitute.

dialogue.text: Scrooge asked if the treadmill and the poor law were still in full vigor.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge asked if the treadmill and the poor law were still in full vigor.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge asked if the treadmill and the poor law were still in full vigor.

dialogue.text: When they replied, yes, Scrooge stated, if they would rather die, they had better do it
dialogue.zh: When they replied, yes, Scrooge stated, if they would rather die, they had better do it
dialogue.ja: When they replied, yes, Scrooge stated, if they would rather die, they had better do it

dialogue.text: and decreased the surplus population.
dialogue.zh: and decreased the surplus population.
dialogue.ja: and decreased the surplus population.

dialogue.text: The gentleman left in despair.
dialogue.zh: The gentleman left in despair.
dialogue.ja: The gentleman left in despair.

dialogue.text: Scrooge worked until it was time to close.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge worked until it was time to close.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge worked until it was time to close.

dialogue.text: You'll want all day tomorrow, I suppose, if quite convenience, sir.
dialogue.zh: You'll want all day tomorrow, I suppose, if quite convenience, sir.
dialogue.ja: You'll want all day tomorrow, I suppose, if quite convenience, sir.

dialogue.text: It's not convenient and it's not fair, but I suppose you must have it.
dialogue.zh: It's not convenient and it's not fair, but I suppose you must have it.
dialogue.ja: It's not convenient and it's not fair, but I suppose you must have it.

dialogue.text: Be here all the earlier next morning.
dialogue.zh: Be here all the earlier next morning.
dialogue.ja: Be here all the earlier next morning.

dialogue.text: I will, sir.
dialogue.zh: I will, sir.
dialogue.ja: I will, sir.

dialogue.text: Scrooge went home to his gloomy old chambers.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge went home to his gloomy old chambers.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge went home to his gloomy old chambers.

dialogue.text: As he put his key in the lock, he saw, instead of the door knocker, the face of Jacob Marley.
dialogue.zh: As he put his key in the lock, he saw, instead of the door knocker, the face of Jacob Marley.
dialogue.ja: As he put his key in the lock, he saw, instead of the door knocker, the face of Jacob Marley.

dialogue.text: It had a dismal light about it, like a bad lobster in a dark cellar.
dialogue.zh: It had a dismal light about it, like a bad lobster in a dark cellar.
dialogue.ja: It had a dismal light about it, like a bad lobster in a dark cellar.

dialogue.text: Scrooge blinked and the knocker was a knocker again.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge blinked and the knocker was a knocker again.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge blinked and the knocker was a knocker again.

dialogue.text: Inside, sitting down to his gruel, he was startled by a ringing sound from the deep cellar below,
dialogue.zh: Inside, sitting down to his gruel, he was startled by a ringing sound from the deep cellar below,
dialogue.ja: Inside, sitting down to his gruel, he was startled by a ringing sound from the deep cellar below,

dialogue.text: a clanking dreadfully disturbing noise.
dialogue.zh: a clanking dreadfully disturbing noise.
dialogue.ja: a clanking dreadfully disturbing noise.

dialogue.text: Then a ghost came in and stood before him.
dialogue.zh: Then a ghost came in and stood before him.
dialogue.ja: Then a ghost came in and stood before him.

dialogue.text: It was Jacob Marley, but dragging an immense heavy chain forged of cash boxes, keys,
dialogue.zh: It was Jacob Marley, but dragging an immense heavy chain forged of cash boxes, keys,
dialogue.ja: It was Jacob Marley, but dragging an immense heavy chain forged of cash boxes, keys,

dialogue.text: ledges, deeds, and heavy purses wrought in steel.
dialogue.zh: ledges, deeds, and heavy purses wrought in steel.
dialogue.ja: ledges, deeds, and heavy purses wrought in steel.

dialogue.text: Marley's face was awful.
dialogue.zh: Marley's face was awful.
dialogue.ja: Marley's face was awful.

dialogue.text: Who are you?
dialogue.zh: Who are you?
dialogue.ja: Who are you?

dialogue.text: In life, I was your partner, Jacob Marley.
dialogue.zh: In life, I was your partner, Jacob Marley.
dialogue.ja: In life, I was your partner, Jacob Marley.

dialogue.text: This is the chain I have forged in life.
dialogue.zh: This is the chain I have forged in life.
dialogue.ja: This is the chain I have forged in life.

dialogue.text: Link by link yard by yard.
dialogue.zh: Link by link yard by yard.
dialogue.ja: Link by link yard by yard.

dialogue.text: Your own train is pumped heavier.
dialogue.zh: Your own train is pumped heavier.
dialogue.ja: Your own train is pumped heavier.

dialogue.text: For you have had seven more years since I death took forged it.
dialogue.zh: For you have had seven more years since I death took forged it.
dialogue.ja: For you have had seven more years since I death took forged it.

dialogue.text: I forged my link by link through green and worldly care.
dialogue.zh: I forged my link by link through green and worldly care.
dialogue.ja: I forged my link by link through green and worldly care.

dialogue.text: I am doomed to wander the earth, witnessing human scenes where I can no longer interfere.
dialogue.zh: I am doomed to wander the earth, witnessing human scenes where I can no longer interfere.
dialogue.ja: I am doomed to wander the earth, witnessing human scenes where I can no longer interfere.

dialogue.text: My sole mission is to warn you, Scrooge.
dialogue.zh: My sole mission is to warn you, Scrooge.
dialogue.ja: My sole mission is to warn you, Scrooge.

dialogue.text: You will be haunted by three spirits.
dialogue.zh: You will be haunted by three spirits.
dialogue.ja: You will be haunted by three spirits.

dialogue.text: The first will arrive when the bell tones won.
dialogue.zh: The first will arrive when the bell tones won.
dialogue.ja: The first will arrive when the bell tones won.

dialogue.text: The second on the next night at the same hour.
dialogue.zh: The second on the next night at the same hour.
dialogue.ja: The second on the next night at the same hour.

dialogue.text: And the third on the night after when the last stroke of twelve ceases.
dialogue.zh: And the third on the night after when the last stroke of twelve ceases.
dialogue.ja: And the third on the night after when the last stroke of twelve ceases.

dialogue.text: Marley floated out, where the air was filled with mournful, restless
dialogue.zh: Marley floated out, where the air was filled with mournful, restless
dialogue.ja: Marley floated out, where the air was filled with mournful, restless

dialogue.text: fountains, each dragging a similar clanking chain.
dialogue.zh: fountains, each dragging a similar clanking chain.
dialogue.ja: fountains, each dragging a similar clanking chain.

dialogue.text: Scrooge staggered back to his bed, locked the door, and went to sleep.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge staggered back to his bed, locked the door, and went to sleep.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge staggered back to his bed, locked the door, and went to sleep.

dialogue.text: Filled with a great, though still skeptical.
dialogue.zh: Filled with a great, though still skeptical.
dialogue.ja: Filled with a great, though still skeptical.

dialogue.text: My red.
dialogue.zh: My red.
dialogue.ja: My red.

dialogue.text: Scrooge awoke precisely at twelve.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge awoke precisely at twelve.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge awoke precisely at twelve.

dialogue.text: He waited nervously.
dialogue.zh: He waited nervously.
dialogue.ja: He waited nervously.

dialogue.text: And when the clock struck one, the curtains of his bed rustled.
dialogue.zh: And when the clock struck one, the curtains of his bed rustled.
dialogue.ja: And when the clock struck one, the curtains of his bed rustled.

dialogue.text: Are you the spirit, sir, whose coming was foretold to me?
dialogue.zh: Are you the spirit, sir, whose coming was foretold to me?
dialogue.ja: Are you the spirit, sir, whose coming was foretold to me?

dialogue.text: I am the ghost of Christmas past.
dialogue.zh: I am the ghost of Christmas past.
dialogue.ja: I am the ghost of Christmas past.

dialogue.text: The spirit commanded Scrooge to rise and walk with it.
dialogue.zh: The spirit commanded Scrooge to rise and walk with it.
dialogue.ja: The spirit commanded Scrooge to rise and walk with it.

dialogue.text: It touched Scrooge's heart, and they passed instantaneously through the walls
dialogue.zh: It touched Scrooge's heart, and they passed instantaneously through the walls
dialogue.ja: It touched Scrooge's heart, and they passed instantaneously through the walls

dialogue.text: and out onto a country road where Scrooge had been a boy.
dialogue.zh: and out onto a country road where Scrooge had been a boy.
dialogue.ja: and out onto a country road where Scrooge had been a boy.

dialogue.text: The spirit showed him his old boarding school, cheerless and deserted,
dialogue.zh: The spirit showed him his old boarding school, cheerless and deserted,
dialogue.ja: The spirit showed him his old boarding school, cheerless and deserted,

dialogue.text: save for one solitary boy reading by a feeble fire.
dialogue.zh: save for one solitary boy reading by a feeble fire.
dialogue.ja: save for one solitary boy reading by a feeble fire.

dialogue.text: Ebenezer Scrooge alone.
dialogue.zh: Ebenezer Scrooge alone.
dialogue.ja: Ebenezer Scrooge alone.

dialogue.text: The scene changed.
dialogue.zh: The scene changed.
dialogue.ja: The scene changed.

dialogue.text: Scrooge saw his younger sister, Phan.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge saw his younger sister, Phan.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge saw his younger sister, Phan.

dialogue.text: I missed you so much, and I have come to bring you home to your brother.
dialogue.zh: I missed you so much, and I have come to bring you home to your brother.
dialogue.ja: I missed you so much, and I have come to bring you home to your brother.

dialogue.text: Father is so much kinder now, and we are to be together all the Christmas long.
dialogue.zh: Father is so much kinder now, and we are to be together all the Christmas long.
dialogue.ja: Father is so much kinder now, and we are to be together all the Christmas long.

dialogue.text: Phan was the mother of his nephew, Fred, and died young.
dialogue.zh: Phan was the mother of his nephew, Fred, and died young.
dialogue.ja: Phan was the mother of his nephew, Fred, and died young.

dialogue.text: The spirit transported him again to a bustling warehouse where he was an apprentice.
dialogue.zh: The spirit transported him again to a bustling warehouse where he was an apprentice.
dialogue.ja: The spirit transported him again to a bustling warehouse where he was an apprentice.

dialogue.text: His master, Fezywig, closed the shop for the night, shouting.
dialogue.zh: His master, Fezywig, closed the shop for the night, shouting.
dialogue.ja: His master, Fezywig, closed the shop for the night, shouting.

dialogue.text: Yo, ho, my boys! No more work tonight!
dialogue.zh: Yo, ho, my boys! No more work tonight!
dialogue.ja: Yo, ho, my boys! No more work tonight!

dialogue.text: Christmas Eve!
dialogue.zh: Christmas Eve!
dialogue.ja: Christmas Eve!

dialogue.text: Fezywig then presided over a grand Christmas party, a fiddle struck up.
dialogue.zh: Fezywig then presided over a grand Christmas party, a fiddle struck up.
dialogue.ja: Fezywig then presided over a grand Christmas party, a fiddle struck up.

dialogue.text: The tea and cakes were passed around, and the warehouse was filled with joyous dancing.
dialogue.zh: The tea and cakes were passed around, and the warehouse was filled with joyous dancing.
dialogue.ja: The tea and cakes were passed around, and the warehouse was filled with joyous dancing.

dialogue.text: Scrooge watched his former self delight in the festivity.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge watched his former self delight in the festivity.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge watched his former self delight in the festivity.

dialogue.text: He has spent but a few pounds of your mortal money, and you praise him as if he were a benefactor of mankind.
dialogue.zh: He has spent but a few pounds of your mortal money, and you praise him as if he were a benefactor of mankind.
dialogue.ja: He has spent but a few pounds of your mortal money, and you praise him as if he were a benefactor of mankind.

dialogue.text: The happiness he gives is quite as great as if it cost a fortune.
dialogue.zh: The happiness he gives is quite as great as if it cost a fortune.
dialogue.ja: The happiness he gives is quite as great as if it cost a fortune.

dialogue.text: The word struck Scrooge's ear, and he hung his head, for he was thinking of Bob Cratchit.
dialogue.zh: The word struck Scrooge's ear, and he hung his head, for he was thinking of Bob Cratchit.
dialogue.ja: The word struck Scrooge's ear, and he hung his head, for he was thinking of Bob Cratchit.

dialogue.text: The scenes became more painful.
dialogue.zh: The scenes became more painful.
dialogue.ja: The scenes became more painful.

dialogue.text: Scrooge saw himself as a young, striving man, with another person seated beside him, a beautiful young woman, Belle.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge saw himself as a young, striving man, with another person seated beside him, a beautiful young woman, Belle.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge saw himself as a young, striving man, with another person seated beside him, a beautiful young woman, Belle.

dialogue.text: It matters little to you, very little.
dialogue.zh: It matters little to you, very little.
dialogue.ja: It matters little to you, very little.

dialogue.text: Another idol has displaced me, a golden one.
dialogue.zh: Another idol has displaced me, a golden one.
dialogue.ja: Another idol has displaced me, a golden one.

dialogue.text: My dear Belle, please understand that all my efforts are for our future.
dialogue.zh: My dear Belle, please understand that all my efforts are for our future.
dialogue.ja: My dear Belle, please understand that all my efforts are for our future.

dialogue.text: I have seen your nobler aspirations fail, and your love of gain grows strong.
dialogue.zh: I have seen your nobler aspirations fail, and your love of gain grows strong.
dialogue.ja: I have seen your nobler aspirations fail, and your love of gain grows strong.

dialogue.text: I'm sorry, Scrooge, but I can't do this anymore.
dialogue.zh: I'm sorry, Scrooge, but I can't do this anymore.
dialogue.ja: I'm sorry, Scrooge, but I can't do this anymore.

dialogue.text: She dissolved their engagement.
dialogue.zh: She dissolved their engagement.
dialogue.ja: She dissolved their engagement.

dialogue.text: The old Scrooge groaned, seeing his greatest regret.
dialogue.zh: The old Scrooge groaned, seeing his greatest regret.
dialogue.ja: The old Scrooge groaned, seeing his greatest regret.

dialogue.text: Finally, the spirit showed him a home scene years later.
dialogue.zh: Finally, the spirit showed him a home scene years later.
dialogue.ja: Finally, the spirit showed him a home scene years later.

dialogue.text: Belle was now a mother, surrounded by happy children and a kind husband.
dialogue.zh: Belle was now a mother, surrounded by happy children and a kind husband.
dialogue.ja: Belle was now a mother, surrounded by happy children and a kind husband.

dialogue.text: Scrooge could bear no more.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge could bear no more.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge could bear no more.

dialogue.text: The ghost remained impassive.
dialogue.zh: The ghost remained impassive.
dialogue.ja: The ghost remained impassive.

dialogue.text: Scrooge wrestled with the spirit, and with a sudden force of desperation, he extinguished the light emanating from its head with its own cap.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge wrestled with the spirit, and with a sudden force of desperation, he extinguished the light emanating from its head with its own cap.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge wrestled with the spirit, and with a sudden force of desperation, he extinguished the light emanating from its head with its own cap.

dialogue.text: He found himself back in his own bed, exhausted, falling into a heavy slumber.
dialogue.zh: He found himself back in his own bed, exhausted, falling into a heavy slumber.
dialogue.ja: He found himself back in his own bed, exhausted, falling into a heavy slumber.

dialogue.text: Scrooge awoke as the clock struck one.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge awoke as the clock struck one.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge awoke as the clock struck one.

dialogue.text: He was now prepared and waited for the next spirit.
dialogue.zh: He was now prepared and waited for the next spirit.
dialogue.ja: He was now prepared and waited for the next spirit.

dialogue.text: A mighty voice called his name, and a flood of light shone from the adjoining room.
dialogue.zh: A mighty voice called his name, and a flood of light shone from the adjoining room.
dialogue.ja: A mighty voice called his name, and a flood of light shone from the adjoining room.

dialogue.text: Scrooge entered to find a most extraordinary sight.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge entered to find a most extraordinary sight.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge entered to find a most extraordinary sight.

dialogue.text: The room was transformed into a paradise of Christmas food.
dialogue.zh: The room was transformed into a paradise of Christmas food.
dialogue.ja: The room was transformed into a paradise of Christmas food.

dialogue.text: Enthroned upon this mountain of food sat a giant spirit glorious to see.
dialogue.zh: Enthroned upon this mountain of food sat a giant spirit glorious to see.
dialogue.ja: Enthroned upon this mountain of food sat a giant spirit glorious to see.

dialogue.text: It bore a glowing torch shaped like a horn of plenty, and wore a simple green robe bordered with white fur.
dialogue.zh: It bore a glowing torch shaped like a horn of plenty, and wore a simple green robe bordered with white fur.
dialogue.ja: It bore a glowing torch shaped like a horn of plenty, and wore a simple green robe bordered with white fur.

dialogue.text: The spirit had more than 1800 brothers, one for every year since the birth of Christ.
dialogue.zh: The spirit had more than 1800 brothers, one for every year since the birth of Christ.
dialogue.ja: The spirit had more than 1800 brothers, one for every year since the birth of Christ.

dialogue.text: The spirit carried Scrooge out into the streets, where people were mirrorless scraping the snow and carrying their dinners.
dialogue.zh: The spirit carried Scrooge out into the streets, where people were mirrorless scraping the snow and carrying their dinners.
dialogue.ja: The spirit carried Scrooge out into the streets, where people were mirrorless scraping the snow and carrying their dinners.

dialogue.text: The spirit sprinkled incense from its torch upon them, which caused any sourness to vanish instantly.
dialogue.zh: The spirit sprinkled incense from its torch upon them, which caused any sourness to vanish instantly.
dialogue.ja: The spirit sprinkled incense from its torch upon them, which caused any sourness to vanish instantly.

dialogue.text: They flew to the poor dwelling of Bob Cratchit.
dialogue.zh: They flew to the poor dwelling of Bob Cratchit.
dialogue.ja: They flew to the poor dwelling of Bob Cratchit.

dialogue.text: They found Mrs Cratchit and her children preparing the modest Christmas feast.
dialogue.zh: They found Mrs Cratchit and her children preparing the modest Christmas feast.
dialogue.ja: They found Mrs Cratchit and her children preparing the modest Christmas feast.

dialogue.text: The two young cratchits came running in, boasting that they smelled the goose.
dialogue.zh: The two young cratchits came running in, boasting that they smelled the goose.
dialogue.ja: The two young cratchits came running in, boasting that they smelled the goose.

dialogue.text: Then came Bob Cratchit himself, carrying his youngest son, Tiny Tim.
dialogue.zh: Then came Bob Cratchit himself, carrying his youngest son, Tiny Tim.
dialogue.ja: Then came Bob Cratchit himself, carrying his youngest son, Tiny Tim.

dialogue.text: Tiny Tim was a cripple.
dialogue.zh: Tiny Tim was a cripple.
dialogue.ja: Tiny Tim was a cripple.

dialogue.text: He bore a little crutch and wore iron frames upon his limbs.
dialogue.zh: He bore a little crutch and wore iron frames upon his limbs.
dialogue.ja: He bore a little crutch and wore iron frames upon his limbs.

dialogue.text: The family sat down to the tiny goose, which they magnified with praise.
dialogue.zh: The family sat down to the tiny goose, which they magnified with praise.
dialogue.ja: The family sat down to the tiny goose, which they magnified with praise.

dialogue.text: Bob offered a toast.
dialogue.zh: Bob offered a toast.
dialogue.ja: Bob offered a toast.

dialogue.text: Scrooge was deeply moved.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge was deeply moved.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge was deeply moved.

dialogue.text: Tell me if Tiny Tim will live.
dialogue.zh: Tell me if Tiny Tim will live.
dialogue.ja: Tell me if Tiny Tim will live.

dialogue.text: The spirit quoted Scrooge's own words.
dialogue.zh: The spirit quoted Scrooge's own words.
dialogue.ja: The spirit quoted Scrooge's own words.

dialogue.text: If he be like to die, he had better do it, and decrease the surplus population.
dialogue.zh: If he be like to die, he had better do it, and decrease the surplus population.
dialogue.ja: If he be like to die, he had better do it, and decrease the surplus population.

dialogue.text: Scrooge hung his head in penitence.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge hung his head in penitence.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge hung his head in penitence.

dialogue.text: The spirit took Scrooge on a journey around the world, to miners gathered around a fire,
dialogue.zh: The spirit took Scrooge on a journey around the world, to miners gathered around a fire,
dialogue.ja: The spirit took Scrooge on a journey around the world, to miners gathered around a fire,

dialogue.text: to lighthouse kilos on a rock, and to sailors far at sea, all observing Christmas with cheer.
dialogue.zh: to lighthouse kilos on a rock, and to sailors far at sea, all observing Christmas with cheer.
dialogue.ja: to lighthouse kilos on a rock, and to sailors far at sea, all observing Christmas with cheer.

dialogue.text: Finally, they landed at the home of Scrooge's nephew Fred.
dialogue.zh: Finally, they landed at the home of Scrooge's nephew Fred.
dialogue.ja: Finally, they landed at the home of Scrooge's nephew Fred.

dialogue.text: Fred was laughing heartily, and his guests were laughing with him.
dialogue.zh: Fred was laughing heartily, and his guests were laughing with him.
dialogue.ja: Fred was laughing heartily, and his guests were laughing with him.

dialogue.text: They were discussing Scrooge, who Fred insisted, deserved pity, not anger.
dialogue.zh: They were discussing Scrooge, who Fred insisted, deserved pity, not anger.
dialogue.ja: They were discussing Scrooge, who Fred insisted, deserved pity, not anger.

dialogue.text: Fred proposed a toast to his uncle.
dialogue.zh: Fred proposed a toast to his uncle.
dialogue.ja: Fred proposed a toast to his uncle.

dialogue.text: He drank to him merrily.
dialogue.zh: He drank to him merrily.
dialogue.ja: He drank to him merrily.

dialogue.text: Scrooge was so softened that he almost offered to join in the toast,
dialogue.zh: Scrooge was so softened that he almost offered to join in the toast,
dialogue.ja: Scrooge was so softened that he almost offered to join in the toast,

dialogue.text: but the spirit pulled him away.
dialogue.zh: but the spirit pulled him away.
dialogue.ja: but the spirit pulled him away.

dialogue.text: The hour grew late.
dialogue.zh: The hour grew late.
dialogue.ja: The hour grew late.

dialogue.text: The ghost of Christmas present grew suddenly old.
dialogue.zh: The ghost of Christmas present grew suddenly old.
dialogue.ja: The ghost of Christmas present grew suddenly old.

dialogue.text: Two pitiful, wretched children, a boy and a girl emerged from beneath its road.
dialogue.zh: Two pitiful, wretched children, a boy and a girl emerged from beneath its road.
dialogue.ja: Two pitiful, wretched children, a boy and a girl emerged from beneath its road.

dialogue.text: The boy is ignorance.
dialogue.zh: The boy is ignorance.
dialogue.ja: The boy is ignorance.

dialogue.text: The girl is want.
dialogue.zh: The girl is want.
dialogue.ja: The girl is want.

dialogue.text: Beware them both, but most of all, beware this boy.
dialogue.zh: Beware them both, but most of all, beware this boy.
dialogue.ja: Beware them both, but most of all, beware this boy.

dialogue.text: For on his brow, I see that written, which is doom, unless the writing be erased.
dialogue.zh: For on his brow, I see that written, which is doom, unless the writing be erased.
dialogue.ja: For on his brow, I see that written, which is doom, unless the writing be erased.

dialogue.text: The clock struck twelve, and as the last stroke ended, the spirit vanished.
dialogue.zh: The clock struck twelve, and as the last stroke ended, the spirit vanished.
dialogue.ja: The clock struck twelve, and as the last stroke ended, the spirit vanished.

dialogue.text: A solemn dark phantom shrouded in a deep black garment came gliding in.
dialogue.zh: A solemn dark phantom shrouded in a deep black garment came gliding in.
dialogue.ja: A solemn dark phantom shrouded in a deep black garment came gliding in.

dialogue.text: The phantom came upon Scrooge slowly, silently and gravely.
dialogue.zh: The phantom came upon Scrooge slowly, silently and gravely.
dialogue.ja: The phantom came upon Scrooge slowly, silently and gravely.

dialogue.text: Scrooge trembled, for he felt the spirit's power,
dialogue.zh: Scrooge trembled, for he felt the spirit's power,
dialogue.ja: Scrooge trembled, for he felt the spirit's power,

dialogue.text: though he could see nothing but a ghostly hand and the folds of its black attire.
dialogue.zh: though he could see nothing but a ghostly hand and the folds of its black attire.
dialogue.ja: though he could see nothing but a ghostly hand and the folds of its black attire.

dialogue.text: Are you the ghost of Christmas yet to come?
dialogue.zh: Are you the ghost of Christmas yet to come?
dialogue.ja: Are you the ghost of Christmas yet to come?

dialogue.text: Tell me that I may yet change these shadows you have shown me.
dialogue.zh: Tell me that I may yet change these shadows you have shown me.
dialogue.ja: Tell me that I may yet change these shadows you have shown me.

dialogue.text: The spirit neither spoke nor moved.
dialogue.zh: The spirit neither spoke nor moved.
dialogue.ja: The spirit neither spoke nor moved.

dialogue.text: The spirit pointed forward with its hand.
dialogue.zh: The spirit pointed forward with its hand.
dialogue.ja: The spirit pointed forward with its hand.

dialogue.text: They were in the city near the exchange,
dialogue.zh: They were in the city near the exchange,
dialogue.ja: They were in the city near the exchange,

dialogue.text: where a group of businessmen were discussing the death of an extremely rich man,
dialogue.zh: where a group of businessmen were discussing the death of an extremely rich man,
dialogue.ja: where a group of businessmen were discussing the death of an extremely rich man,

dialogue.text: who was so despised that no one would attend his funeral.
dialogue.zh: who was so despised that no one would attend his funeral.
dialogue.ja: who was so despised that no one would attend his funeral.

dialogue.text: Scrooge wondered who this dead man was, but the spirit gave no sign.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge wondered who this dead man was, but the spirit gave no sign.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge wondered who this dead man was, but the spirit gave no sign.

dialogue.text: They were next in a wretched part of London, at an infamous rag and bone shot.
dialogue.zh: They were next in a wretched part of London, at an infamous rag and bone shot.
dialogue.ja: They were next in a wretched part of London, at an infamous rag and bone shot.

dialogue.text: Three people had gathered to sell items stolen from the dead man's house,
dialogue.zh: Three people had gathered to sell items stolen from the dead man's house,
dialogue.ja: Three people had gathered to sell items stolen from the dead man's house,

dialogue.text: including a shirt, a blanket, and even the very curtains from around his bed.
dialogue.zh: including a shirt, a blanket, and even the very curtains from around his bed.
dialogue.ja: including a shirt, a blanket, and even the very curtains from around his bed.

dialogue.text: Scrooge was horrified by this utter lack of pity for the deceased man.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge was horrified by this utter lack of pity for the deceased man.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge was horrified by this utter lack of pity for the deceased man.

dialogue.text: The ghost silently led Scrooge to a bed chamber in the dead man's house.
dialogue.zh: The ghost silently led Scrooge to a bed chamber in the dead man's house.
dialogue.ja: The ghost silently led Scrooge to a bed chamber in the dead man's house.

dialogue.text: The room was dark, and on the miserable, uncurdened bed lay a lonely, unwatched body covered by a sheet.
dialogue.zh: The room was dark, and on the miserable, uncurdened bed lay a lonely, unwatched body covered by a sheet.
dialogue.ja: The room was dark, and on the miserable, uncurdened bed lay a lonely, unwatched body covered by a sheet.

dialogue.text: Scrooge dared not lift the corner of the sheet to see the face.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge dared not lift the corner of the sheet to see the face.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge dared not lift the corner of the sheet to see the face.

dialogue.text: Ghost, I see, I see. The case of this unhappy man might be my own.
dialogue.zh: Ghost, I see, I see. The case of this unhappy man might be my own.
dialogue.ja: Ghost, I see, I see. The case of this unhappy man might be my own.

dialogue.text: My life tends that way now.
dialogue.zh: My life tends that way now.
dialogue.ja: My life tends that way now.

dialogue.text: They left the chamber of death, and were transported to a poor humble house,
dialogue.zh: They left the chamber of death, and were transported to a poor humble house,
dialogue.ja: They left the chamber of death, and were transported to a poor humble house,

dialogue.text: where a couple, the Dilber family, was rejoicing with a tearful relief.
dialogue.zh: where a couple, the Dilber family, was rejoicing with a tearful relief.
dialogue.ja: where a couple, the Dilber family, was rejoicing with a tearful relief.

dialogue.text: They owed money to a stern creditor, and with his death, they had a day's grace to gather the payment,
dialogue.zh: They owed money to a stern creditor, and with his death, they had a day's grace to gather the payment,
dialogue.ja: They owed money to a stern creditor, and with his death, they had a day's grace to gather the payment,

dialogue.text: a small reprieve from ruin.
dialogue.zh: a small reprieve from ruin.
dialogue.ja: a small reprieve from ruin.

dialogue.text: The dead man's name was never mentioned, yet the effect of his life on others was clear.
dialogue.zh: The dead man's name was never mentioned, yet the effect of his life on others was clear.
dialogue.ja: The dead man's name was never mentioned, yet the effect of his life on others was clear.

dialogue.text: Finally, the spirit took him to Bob Cratchit's home.
dialogue.zh: Finally, the spirit took him to Bob Cratchit's home.
dialogue.ja: Finally, the spirit took him to Bob Cratchit's home.

dialogue.text: The family was quietly morning. Bob was late, having walked slowly.
dialogue.zh: The family was quietly morning. Bob was late, having walked slowly.
dialogue.ja: The family was quietly morning. Bob was late, having walked slowly.

dialogue.text: He explained that he had just been to the place where tiny Tim lay at rest.
dialogue.zh: He explained that he had just been to the place where tiny Tim lay at rest.
dialogue.ja: He explained that he had just been to the place where tiny Tim lay at rest.

dialogue.text: Mrs Cratchit was weeping silently, and Bob tried to comfort them, remembering Tim's patience and goodness.
dialogue.zh: Mrs Cratchit was weeping silently, and Bob tried to comfort them, remembering Tim's patience and goodness.
dialogue.ja: Mrs Cratchit was weeping silently, and Bob tried to comfort them, remembering Tim's patience and goodness.

dialogue.text: Scrooge broke down.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge broke down.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge broke down.

dialogue.text: He saw his own name upon a gravestone, Ebenezer Scrooge.
dialogue.zh: He saw his own name upon a gravestone, Ebenezer Scrooge.
dialogue.ja: He saw his own name upon a gravestone, Ebenezer Scrooge.

dialogue.text: Scrooge fell to his knees before the phantom.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge fell to his knees before the phantom.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge fell to his knees before the phantom.

dialogue.text: Specter, something informs me that our parting moment is at hand.
dialogue.zh: Specter, something informs me that our parting moment is at hand.
dialogue.ja: Specter, something informs me that our parting moment is at hand.

dialogue.text: I know that man lying upon the bed was myself.
dialogue.zh: I know that man lying upon the bed was myself.
dialogue.ja: I know that man lying upon the bed was myself.

dialogue.text: Why show me this, if I am past all hope?
dialogue.zh: Why show me this, if I am past all hope?
dialogue.ja: Why show me this, if I am past all hope?

dialogue.text: I will honor Christmas in my heart, and try to keep it all the year.
dialogue.zh: I will honor Christmas in my heart, and try to keep it all the year.
dialogue.ja: I will honor Christmas in my heart, and try to keep it all the year.

dialogue.text: I will live in the past, the present, and the future.
dialogue.zh: I will live in the past, the present, and the future.
dialogue.ja: I will live in the past, the present, and the future.

dialogue.text: The spirits shall strive within me. I will not shut out the lessons that they teach.
dialogue.zh: The spirits shall strive within me. I will not shut out the lessons that they teach.
dialogue.ja: The spirits shall strive within me. I will not shut out the lessons that they teach.

dialogue.text: He reached out to Clutch the spirit's hand, but the phantom's hood and dress, shrunk, collapse.
dialogue.zh: He reached out to Clutch the spirit's hand, but the phantom's hood and dress, shrunk, collapse.
dialogue.ja: He reached out to Clutch the spirit's hand, but the phantom's hood and dress, shrunk, collapse.

dialogue.text: And dwindled down into a bedpost.
dialogue.zh: And dwindled down into a bedpost.
dialogue.ja: And dwindled down into a bedpost.

dialogue.text: Ha, ha, Scrooge awoke in his own bed, clutching the bedpost, which was, in fact, his own.
dialogue.zh: Ha, ha, Scrooge awoke in his own bed, clutching the bedpost, which was, in fact, his own.
dialogue.ja: Ha, ha, Scrooge awoke in his own bed, clutching the bedpost, which was, in fact, his own.

dialogue.text: They are not gone.
dialogue.zh: They are not gone.
dialogue.ja: They are not gone.

dialogue.text: The spirits have done it all in one night.
dialogue.zh: The spirits have done it all in one night.
dialogue.ja: The spirits have done it all in one night.

dialogue.text: I don't know what day of the month it is. I don't know how long I have been among the spirit.
dialogue.zh: I don't know what day of the month it is. I don't know how long I have been among the spirit.
dialogue.ja: I don't know what day of the month it is. I don't know how long I have been among the spirit.

dialogue.text: I don't know anything, I'm quite a baby.
dialogue.zh: I don't know anything, I'm quite a baby.
dialogue.ja: I don't know anything, I'm quite a baby.

dialogue.text: He danced about the room laughing and weeping at the same time.
dialogue.zh: He danced about the room laughing and weeping at the same time.
dialogue.ja: He danced about the room laughing and weeping at the same time.

dialogue.text: He looked out the window.
dialogue.zh: He looked out the window.
dialogue.ja: He looked out the window.

dialogue.text: A boy was passing by in his Sunday clothes.
dialogue.zh: A boy was passing by in his Sunday clothes.
dialogue.ja: A boy was passing by in his Sunday clothes.

dialogue.text: Ha, what's today my fine fellow?
dialogue.zh: Ha, what's today my fine fellow?
dialogue.ja: Ha, what's today my fine fellow?

dialogue.text: Today, why Christmas Day?
dialogue.zh: Today, why Christmas Day?
dialogue.ja: Today, why Christmas Day?

dialogue.text: He hired the boy to go to the poultry shop and purchase the largest prize-winning turkey they had,
dialogue.zh: He hired the boy to go to the poultry shop and purchase the largest prize-winning turkey they had,
dialogue.ja: He hired the boy to go to the poultry shop and purchase the largest prize-winning turkey they had,

dialogue.text: and have it delivered to Bob Cratchit's house immediately.
dialogue.zh: and have it delivered to Bob Cratchit's house immediately.
dialogue.ja: and have it delivered to Bob Cratchit's house immediately.

dialogue.text: He chuckled at the idea of Bob knowing who sent it.
dialogue.zh: He chuckled at the idea of Bob knowing who sent it.
dialogue.ja: He chuckled at the idea of Bob knowing who sent it.

dialogue.text: Scrooge dressed in his best clothes.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge dressed in his best clothes.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge dressed in his best clothes.

dialogue.text: He was leaving his room when he encountered one of the portley gentlemen
dialogue.zh: He was leaving his room when he encountered one of the portley gentlemen
dialogue.ja: He was leaving his room when he encountered one of the portley gentlemen

dialogue.text: who had solicited charity the day before.
dialogue.zh: who had solicited charity the day before.
dialogue.ja: who had solicited charity the day before.

dialogue.text: My dear sir, a great many back payments are included in it, I assure you.
dialogue.zh: My dear sir, a great many back payments are included in it, I assure you.
dialogue.ja: My dear sir, a great many back payments are included in it, I assure you.

dialogue.text: Will you do me that favor?
dialogue.zh: Will you do me that favor?
dialogue.ja: Will you do me that favor?

dialogue.text: He whispered the amount, and the gentleman was struck dumb with astonishment at the enormous sum.
dialogue.zh: He whispered the amount, and the gentleman was struck dumb with astonishment at the enormous sum.
dialogue.ja: He whispered the amount, and the gentleman was struck dumb with astonishment at the enormous sum.

dialogue.text: He walked out into the street, everything delighted him.
dialogue.zh: He walked out into the street, everything delighted him.
dialogue.ja: He walked out into the street, everything delighted him.

dialogue.text: The cheerful ringing of the bells, the people hurrying along with smiling faces,
dialogue.zh: The cheerful ringing of the bells, the people hurrying along with smiling faces,
dialogue.ja: The cheerful ringing of the bells, the people hurrying along with smiling faces,

dialogue.text: the very air itself.
dialogue.zh: the very air itself.
dialogue.ja: the very air itself.

dialogue.text: Seeing Fred in the distance, Scrooge crossed the street with a beaming face.
dialogue.zh: Seeing Fred in the distance, Scrooge crossed the street with a beaming face.
dialogue.ja: Seeing Fred in the distance, Scrooge crossed the street with a beaming face.

dialogue.text: He asked if he was still welcome to Christmas dinner, and Freddie said yes.
dialogue.zh: He asked if he was still welcome to Christmas dinner, and Freddie said yes.
dialogue.ja: He asked if he was still welcome to Christmas dinner, and Freddie said yes.

dialogue.text: Scrooge spent a wonderful, joyous day with his nephew, his niece, and their guests.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge spent a wonderful, joyous day with his nephew, his niece, and their guests.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge spent a wonderful, joyous day with his nephew, his niece, and their guests.

dialogue.text: The next morning, Scrooge was in the office long before Bob Cratchit.
dialogue.zh: The next morning, Scrooge was in the office long before Bob Cratchit.
dialogue.ja: The next morning, Scrooge was in the office long before Bob Cratchit.

dialogue.text: He sat waiting, gloating over the idea of catching him coming in late.
dialogue.zh: He sat waiting, gloating over the idea of catching him coming in late.
dialogue.ja: He sat waiting, gloating over the idea of catching him coming in late.

dialogue.text: Hello, what do you mean by coming here at this time of day?
dialogue.zh: Hello, what do you mean by coming here at this time of day?
dialogue.ja: Hello, what do you mean by coming here at this time of day?

dialogue.text: I am very sorry, sir, I am behind my time.
dialogue.zh: I am very sorry, sir, I am behind my time.
dialogue.ja: I am very sorry, sir, I am behind my time.

dialogue.text: Now I'll tell you what, my friend.
dialogue.zh: Now I'll tell you what, my friend.
dialogue.ja: Now I'll tell you what, my friend.

dialogue.text: I am not going to stand this sort of thing any longer.
dialogue.zh: I am not going to stand this sort of thing any longer.
dialogue.ja: I am not going to stand this sort of thing any longer.

dialogue.text: And therefore I am about to raise your salary.
dialogue.zh: And therefore I am about to raise your salary.
dialogue.ja: And therefore I am about to raise your salary.

dialogue.text: A Merry Christmas, Bob.
dialogue.zh: A Merry Christmas, Bob.
dialogue.ja: A Merry Christmas, Bob.

dialogue.text: A merry Christmas, Bob, then I have given you for many a long year.
dialogue.zh: A merry Christmas, Bob, then I have given you for many a long year.
dialogue.ja: A merry Christmas, Bob, then I have given you for many a long year.

dialogue.text: I shall endeavour to assist your struggling family, and we will discuss your affairs this very afternoon over a bowl of smoking bishop.
dialogue.zh: I shall endeavour to assist your struggling family, and we will discuss your affairs this very afternoon over a bowl of smoking bishop.
dialogue.ja: I shall endeavour to assist your struggling family, and we will discuss your affairs this very afternoon over a bowl of smoking bishop.

dialogue.text: Bob was aghast, believing his master had gone mad.
dialogue.zh: Bob was aghast, believing his master had gone mad.
dialogue.ja: Bob was aghast, believing his master had gone mad.

dialogue.text: Scrooge was better than his word.
dialogue.zh: Scrooge was better than his word.
dialogue.ja: Scrooge was better than his word.

dialogue.text: He did everything he promised, and infinitely more.
dialogue.zh: He did everything he promised, and infinitely more.
dialogue.ja: He did everything he promised, and infinitely more.

dialogue.text: He raised Bob's salary, became a second father to tiny Tim, who did not die.
dialogue.zh: He raised Bob's salary, became a second father to tiny Tim, who did not die.
dialogue.ja: He raised Bob's salary, became a second father to tiny Tim, who did not die.

dialogue.text: And he became as good a friend, as good a master, and as good a man, as the good old city ever knew.
dialogue.zh: And he became as good a friend, as good a master, and as good a man, as the good old city ever knew.
dialogue.ja: And he became as good a friend, as good a master, and as good a man, as the good old city ever knew.

dialogue.text: Some people laughed at his transformation, but Scrooge let them laugh.
dialogue.zh: Some people laughed at his transformation, but Scrooge let them laugh.
dialogue.ja: Some people laughed at his transformation, but Scrooge let them laugh.

dialogue.text: For he knew that nothing ever happened on this earth for good, at which some people did not have their fill of laughter.
dialogue.zh: For he knew that nothing ever happened on this earth for good, at which some people did not have their fill of laughter.
dialogue.ja: For he knew that nothing ever happened on this earth for good, at which some people did not have their fill of laughter.

dialogue.text: Tiny Tim, who grew strong, uttered a favourite saying which Scrooge always understood.
dialogue.zh: Tiny Tim, who grew strong, uttered a favourite saying which Scrooge always understood.
dialogue.ja: Tiny Tim, who grew strong, uttered a favourite saying which Scrooge always understood.

dialogue.text: God bless us, everyone!
dialogue.zh: God bless us, everyone!
dialogue.ja: God bless us, everyone!

dialogue.zh: God bless us!
